behavior: Slow, methodical canopy forager that gleans invertebrates from leaves; adults are often heard giving persistent song.
field_marks: Olive-green upperparts, including back, wings, and tail
Clean white throat, breast, and belly
Gray crown with a bold white eyebrow stripe
Dark eye-line bordering the eyebrow; adult iris dark red but often appears dark at distance
Strong, long dark bill with a small hook at the tip
Stocky body with a fairly short tail and dark gray legs
habitat: Deciduous and mixed forests with dense or shrubby understory; also uses woodland, forest edges, second-growth forest, and other tree-rich habitats during movement periods.

sources.title: Red-eyed Vireo Identification — All About Birds
